global_context.scene_description: A photorealistic outdoor scene of an old, weathered residential outdoor air conditioning condenser unit in the side yard of a Phoenix-style stucco home. The aging AC unit is the dominant subject, framed at slightly low angle so it feels imposing. The unit shows clear signs of years of Arizona sun: faded paint on the metal cabinet, light surface rust on the corner panels, dust caked into the protective grille fins, sun-bleached factory stickers, slightly bent fan guard wire. The unit sits on a small concrete pad with weeds growing through cracks. Behind the unit, a tan stucco wall and a stretch of empty pale Phoenix sky. To one side, the silhouette of a saguaro cactus and palo verde branches in soft focus. The light is warm late-afternoon golden hour, raking from the right, casting long soft shadows behind the unit. Heat shimmer in the air. Mood: this thing has been working harder than it should for too many summers. Photorealistic, shot on a 50mm lens, slight grain.
global_context.weather_atmosphere: warm, dry, dust hanging in air

lighting.direction: from right, raking, casting long shadows

composition.camera_angle: slightly low angle, eye-level with the unit's mid-height, 3/4 view
composition.framing: medium, the AC unit fills the central 50% of the frame, environmental context around it
composition.depth_of_field: deep, AC unit and stucco wall sharp, distant cactus softly out of focus

small spider web in one corner

semantic_relationships: the AC unit is the protagonist, aged and worn, sitting in its own quiet patch of Phoenix yard
warm golden light gives the scene a contemplative tone — not alarming, just honest
no people, no logos, no text — just the system that's been carrying the household through Phoenix summers
